titlestmt.titleproper: Preliminary Guide to the Robin William Winks Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by staff of Manuscripts and Archives

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

conventiondeclaration.abbr: Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual
conventiondeclaration.citation: translation missing: en.enumerations.resource_finding_aid_description_rules.Finding aid created in accordance with Manuscripts and Archives Processing Manual

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday June 3, 2023 at 09:46

did.abstract: The papers consist of correspondence, subject files, writings,and audiotapes documenting the personal life and professional career of Robin William Winks.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Robin Winks, pre-1987-1993, 2000, 2001; gift of Avril Winks, 2004.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Office files and Miscellaneous recommendations, New Haven Colony Historical Society, Fulbright in Accession 2001-M-019 and all of Accession 2002-M-011 is unprocessed and may contain sensitive information or be in a physical state that would prohibit use. Researchers wishing to request access should email mssa.assist@yale.edu requesting specific box numbers in order to initiate the review process, which may take several weeks.

Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy of non-commercially produced items for their personal use should consult the ordering reproductions information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright for unpublished materials authored or otherwise produced by Robin William Winks has been transferred to Yale University. These materials may be used for non-commercial purposes without seeking permission from Yale University as the copyright holder. For other uses of these materials, please contact mssa.assist@yale.edu.

Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Robin William Winks Papers (MS 336). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Robin William Evert Winks was born in Indiana on December 5, 1930. He graduated from the University of Colorado in 1952 and received an M.A. in Maori studies from Victoria University as a Fulbright Scholar in New Zealand. He received his Ph.D. from Johns Hopkins in 1957. Winks joined Yale's history department in 1957, serving as its chairman from 1996 to 1999. From 1999 to 2000, he was the Vyvyan Harmsworth visiting professor of American history at Oxford University. He was a prolific author and in 1987 published Cloak & Gown: Scholars in America's Secret War , an account of the Central Intelligence Agency's relationship with major American universities. Winks died in New Haven, Connecticut, on April 7, 2003.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The papers consist of correspondence, subject files, writings, and audiotapes documenting the personal life and professional career of Robin William Winks.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This accession contains Winks's professional correspondence from his time as a professor, student work, teaching files, conference presentations, and research files. The bulk of the accession consists of drafts, research, and notes for Winks's book, Cloak and Gown: Scholars in America's Secret War , which explored the relationship between universities, particularly Yale, and the Central Intelligence Agency (CIA) and its predecessor, the Office of Strategic Services (OSS). Also included are Winks's undergraduate papers; his teaching files from his time as a professor at Yale; papers written by Yale students in Winks's classes; and research materials for Winks's unpublished biography of William Francis Drummond. The research materials for the unpublished Drummond biography consist primarily of reproductions of documents that Winks used in his research.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This material is arranged in three groupings: Correspondence; Research Files; Teaching Files. Materials within these groupings are arranged in the original order provided by the donor.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 1 contains Yale University personnel files and is restricted until January 1, 2072.

did.unittitle: Association of Yale Alumni trip to Quebec
William Francis Drummond Jervois unpublished biography

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This grouping contains research created by Winks on the life of Sir William Francis Drummond Jervois, a British military engineer and diplomat. Jervois's service in the British military was in Australia, New Zealand, and Southeast Asia, where he served as governor of the Straits Settlement, Southern Australia, and New Zealand between 1875 and 1888.

These research materials were created by Winks in preparation for writing a biography of Jervois, which was not published. The bulk of material in this grouping consists of photocopies and duplications of primary source documents, and dates reflect the dates of the original documents, not of the photocopies. The grouping also includes materials produced by Winks, including notes, correspondence, and short written pieces that discuss either Jervois or the biography.

title.part: Cloak and Gown: Scholars in America's Secret War

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This file includes manuscript of nine chapters on the Office of Strategic Services (OSS) and Central Intelligence Agency (CIA).
